purpose: Implement the missing real runtime surfaces for Neovim/Vim and TUI, based on the already existing contract/foundation layer.
goal: Ship a real runnable Neovim/Vim plugin and a real runnable TUI application that reuse Ananta backend APIs and keep orchestration/governance in the hub.

missing_runtime_evidence: No Neovim Lua runtime plugin files present.
No Vim runtime plugin files in plugin/ or autoload/ present.
No dedicated runnable TUI app package/entrypoint present.
No packaging artifacts for plugin distribution present.
No CI lane that builds and smoke-tests real plugin + TUI runtimes.

success_criteria: Repository contains runnable Neovim/Vim plugin runtime files and documented install/start commands.
Repository contains runnable TUI app entrypoint with core views working against backend APIs.
Both surfaces preserve explicit approval, audit and bounded-context behavior.
Contract layer remains aligned with runtime behavior and is not misrepresented as full implementation.
Todo completion status is backed by executable evidence.
program_success_criteria: Repository contains runnable Neovim/Vim plugin runtime files and documented install/start commands.
Repository contains runnable TUI app entrypoint with core views working against backend APIs.
Both surfaces preserve explicit approval, audit and bounded-context behavior.
Contract layer remains aligned with runtime behavior and is not misrepresented as full implementation.
Todo completion status is backed by executable evidence.
architecture_guardrails: Hub stays control plane; plugin and TUI stay thin clients.
No client-side bypass of approval, governance, audit or mutation gates.
Use additive migration from contracts to runtime implementations, no big-bang rewrite.
Keep browser-first fallback for deep admin/configuration surfaces.

tasks.title: Runtime evidence inventory finalize
acceptance_criteria: Inventory explicitly lists contract-only artifacts and runtime artifacts.
Inventory includes exact file paths for Neovim/Vim/TUI evidence.
Inventory is linked in this track documentation.

tasks.title: Foundation versus runtime status labels normalize
acceptance_criteria: Track terminology distinguishes foundation_complete from runtime_complete.
Current done claims are reconciled against executable evidence.
No runtime claim remains ambiguous.

tasks.title: Completion definition for plugin and TUI formalize
acceptance_criteria: Definition includes required entrypoints, smoke tests and packaging evidence.
Definition includes required degraded/error behavior and audit expectations.
Definition is reusable for future client tracks.

tasks.title: Contract-to-runtime mapping table create
acceptance_criteria: Each existing editor/TUI contract maps to one runtime UI module or is marked deferred.
Mapping identifies missing runtime modules explicitly.
Mapping avoids duplicate responsibilities across client surfaces.

tasks.title: Runtime architecture decision record write
acceptance_criteria: ADR confirms Neovim-first plus Vim compatibility strategy.
ADR selects concrete TUI runtime stack and why.
ADR includes constraints from hub-worker architecture.

tasks.title: Repository layout for plugin and TUI runtime create
acceptance_criteria: Clear directories exist for nvim plugin, vim compatibility and TUI app.
Layout separates runtime code from contract-only services.
Paths are documented for contributors and CI.

tasks.title: Shared client API module implement
acceptance_criteria: Reusable client module handles health/goals/tasks/artifacts/approvals endpoints.
Module supports retries/timeouts with explicit error signaling.
Module is reused by both plugin and TUI runtimes.

tasks.title: Shared auth and session handling implement
acceptance_criteria: Auth/session manager supports configured modes without secret leakage.
Token/session refresh failures become visible in clients.
No client writes secrets to logs or history.

tasks.title: Connection profile persistence harden
acceptance_criteria: Profile persistence restores non-sensitive fields only.
Per-environment profile switching is deterministic.
Corrupt profile state fails safely with repair guidance.

tasks.title: Capability handshake and degraded model implement
acceptance_criteria: Client startup validates backend health and capability availability.
Missing capabilities switch clients into explicit degraded modes.
Degraded mode blocks unsupported actions with clear rationale.

tasks.title: Trace and audit linking helper implement
acceptance_criteria: Client operations propagate trace/task context fields consistently.
UI surfaces can show trace references for troubleshooting.
Audit-sensitive actions include sufficient linkage metadata.

tasks.title: Client action dispatcher implement
acceptance_criteria: Dispatcher supports explicit action types analyze/review/patch/new/evolve.
Dispatcher enforces bounded context payload assembly.
Dispatcher rejects unknown action names fail-closed.

tasks.title: Neovim Lua plugin skeleton add
acceptance_criteria: Runtime files are added under lua/ananta/ with loadable module structure.
Core modules include init, commands, api, state and render helpers.
Plugin loads in headless Neovim without runtime errors.

tasks.title: Plugin entrypoint and commands register
acceptance_criteria: plugin/ananta.vim (or equivalent) registers user commands.
Commands include AnantaGoalSubmit, AnantaAnalyze, AnantaReview, AnantaPatchPlan, AnantaProjectNew, AnantaProjectEvolve.
Command help text and argument behavior are deterministic.

tasks.title: Goal submission command runtime implement
acceptance_criteria: AnantaGoalSubmit sends bounded editor context to backend.
Success and failure outputs are rendered in editor-safe views.
No hidden file mutation is performed.

tasks.title: Analyze review patch commands runtime implement
acceptance_criteria: Analyze/review/patch commands call backend action dispatcher.
Patch command remains review-first with explicit apply path only.
Failures are surfaced with actionable messages.

tasks.title: Editor context capture runtime implement
acceptance_criteria: Selection, file path and buffer excerpts are captured with strict bounds.
Visual-mode and normal-mode captures are both supported.
Users can inspect what context will be sent.

tasks.title: Result rendering surfaces implement
acceptance_criteria: Results can render to scratch buffer and/or floating view.
Long output handling remains readable and navigable.
Render path handles unsupported artifacts with safe fallback.

tasks.title: Task artifact navigation in plugin implement
acceptance_criteria: Plugin supports opening linked task/artifact summaries.
Navigation preserves current editor context where possible.
Browser handoff is available for deep detail surfaces.

tasks.title: Approval awareness and blocked UX in plugin implement
acceptance_criteria: Approval-required and blocked states are clearly visible.
No command path silently executes blocked mutations.
User gets explicit next step guidance for approvals.

tasks.title: First-run setup and diagnostics commands add
acceptance_criteria: Setup command validates endpoint and auth configuration.
Diagnostics command checks health and capability handshake.
Command output is concise and actionable.

tasks.title: Neovim runtime smoke flow script add
acceptance_criteria: Automated or semi-automated script validates plugin load plus one action.
Smoke flow captures artifacts for CI debugging.
Failure blocks runtime_complete claim.

tasks.title: Vim compatibility strategy finalize
acceptance_criteria: Compatibility scope defines what works in Vim versus Neovim only.
Fallback behavior for missing Neovim APIs is explicit.
Strategy avoids hidden behavior divergence.

tasks.title: Vim command wrapper layer implement
acceptance_criteria: Vim commands map to supported backend actions through a safe bridge.
Wrapper layer keeps command names consistent where practical.
Unsupported actions return explicit not-supported messaging.

tasks.title: Neovim-only feature guards implement
acceptance_criteria: Neovim-only features are detected at runtime.
Vim users receive deterministic fallback guidance.
No hard crash due to unavailable APIs.

tasks.title: Vim Neovim support matrix tests add
acceptance_criteria: Matrix tests cover minimum supported Neovim and Vim variants.
Tests verify command registration and graceful degradations.
Matrix expectations are documented for release notes.

tasks.title: Vim compatibility docs add
acceptance_criteria: Docs list supported commands and known limitations by editor.
Setup and troubleshooting are editor-specific where needed.
Docs avoid claiming unsupported parity.

tasks.title: Plugin help tags and runtime docs package
acceptance_criteria: Plugin ships with installable help documentation.
Help includes command reference and safety caveats.
Help text is synchronized with runtime behavior.

tasks.title: TUI app package and entrypoint implement
acceptance_criteria: Dedicated TUI app package exists with runnable entrypoint command.
Entry command starts app loop and handles startup failures cleanly.
Entry command supports configurable backend profile.

tasks.title: TUI framework bootstrap and app shell implement
acceptance_criteria: App shell includes global layout regions and keyboard routing.
View switching and refresh behavior are deterministic.
Terminal-size degradation path is implemented.

tasks.title: Runtime status header implement
acceptance_criteria: Header shows active profile, endpoint and connection health.
Header updates when auth or connectivity changes.
Status labels are concise and unambiguous.

tasks.title: Task board and task detail runtime views implement
acceptance_criteria: Task board supports refresh, selection and grouping baseline.
Task detail view shows summary, status, routing and linked artifacts.
Views handle empty and error states without crashing.

tasks.title: Artifact runtime view and rendering modes implement
acceptance_criteria: Artifact list and selected detail rendering are implemented.
Known types render meaningfully; unknown types degrade to raw/browser fallback.
Rendering never implies automatic mutation.

tasks.title: Goal submission runtime flow in TUI implement
acceptance_criteria: TUI supports explicit goal submission with bounded context.
Goal submission result links into task/artifact navigation.
Validation errors are clear and recoverable.

tasks.title: Approval queue runtime view implement
acceptance_criteria: Approval queue lists pending items with scope and risk hints.
Approval details can be inspected safely.
No hidden approve/reject side effects occur.

tasks.title: Logs and audit runtime view implement
acceptance_criteria: Log stream view supports at least level filtering.
Audit trace references are visible for selected operations.
Sensitive values are redacted in rendered entries.

tasks.title: Connection and profile runtime screen implement
acceptance_criteria: Users can select profile and validate connectivity.
Auth failures show specific remediation hints.
Profile switch updates all dependent views safely.

tasks.title: TUI resume state persistence implement
acceptance_criteria: Last profile and last active view can be restored safely.
Sensitive fields are excluded from persisted state.
Corrupt state fallback path is deterministic.

tasks.title: Mutation-safe client action gates enforce
acceptance_criteria: Plugin and TUI mutation-capable actions require explicit backend approval state.
Disallowed actions fail closed with user-visible reason.
Client-side shortcuts cannot bypass backend policy.

tasks.title: Secret and token redaction layer harden
acceptance_criteria: Runtime logs and UI text sanitize token-like values.
Debug mode does not expose secrets by default.
Redaction behavior is covered by tests.

tasks.title: Error and degraded state UX harden
acceptance_criteria: No data, disconnected, auth failed and permission denied are distinct states.
Each state provides actionable next steps.
Clients avoid silent hangs in degraded conditions.

tasks.title: Context provenance and bounds visibility in runtime UIs add
acceptance_criteria: Runtime UIs show source classes for context payload parts.
Users can inspect bounded payload before submit.
Visibility remains compact and readable.

tasks.title: Minimal optional telemetry for runtime diagnostics add
acceptance_criteria: Telemetry is opt-in and excludes secrets/content payloads.
Trace IDs and operation class are sufficient for diagnostics.
Telemetry can be disabled completely.

tasks.title: Browser fallback policy centralize and enforce
acceptance_criteria: Policy defines which deep screens stay browser-first.
Plugin and TUI use consistent fallback behavior.
Fallback links preserve task/trace context where possible.

tasks.title: Neovim Lua unit tests add
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover command dispatch, context capture and renderer paths.
Tests include error and degraded scenarios.
Tests run headless in CI.

tasks.title: Plugin backend integration tests add
acceptance_criteria: Integration tests use mocked backend endpoints for core commands.
Tests verify auth failure and missing capability behavior.
Tests verify no silent mutation on review-only paths.

tasks.title: TUI component and view-model tests add
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover task, artifact, approval and log view-model logic.
Tests include keyboard navigation and filtering behavior.
Tests cover empty and error state rendering.

tasks.title: Cross-surface smoke workflow tests add
acceptance_criteria: Smoke workflow validates plugin startup plus one action.
Smoke workflow validates TUI startup plus one task/artifact flow.
Smoke outputs are archived for CI debugging.

tasks.title: CI lanes for plugin and TUI runtime add
acceptance_criteria: CI includes jobs for plugin tests and TUI tests.
CI fails on missing runtime files required by completion criteria.
CI publishes smoke artifacts on failure.

tasks.title: Golden render snapshots for major views add
acceptance_criteria: Stable snapshots exist for key plugin and TUI result surfaces.
Snapshots make regressions visible in review.
Snapshots avoid embedding sensitive dynamic content.

tasks.title: Security focused tests for client runtimes add
acceptance_criteria: Tests verify secret redaction and bounded context enforcement.
Tests verify blocked mutation paths fail closed.
Tests verify approval-required paths do not auto-execute.

tasks.title: Contract-runtime consistency checker add
acceptance_criteria: Checker reports contracts that have no runtime implementation.
Checker reports runtime modules missing contract coverage where expected.
Checker output is integrated into release checklist.

tasks.title: Plugin and TUI packaging artifacts ship
acceptance_criteria: Neovim/Vim plugin package artifact is buildable and installable.
TUI package artifact is buildable and installable.
Versioning metadata is consistent across artifacts.

tasks.title: Install and quickstart docs verify against real runtime
acceptance_criteria: Install docs are validated on clean environment.
Quickstart reaches first successful action for plugin and TUI.
Known limitations are clearly documented.

tasks.title: Release checklist and rollback plan add
acceptance_criteria: Checklist includes build, tests, smoke, docs and artifact verification.
Rollback steps exist for bad client release.
Checklist is short enough for practical release cadence.

tasks.title: Status synchronization and evidence report finalize
acceptance_criteria: todo status is updated only after evidence links are attached.
Final report lists executable files, commands and test results.
Track can be audited without relying on implicit assumptions.

execution_order_summary: Stage 1: Reality baseline and completion definitions (TVM-T01..T06)
summary_notes: This track explicitly captures the currently missing runtime implementation surface for Neovim/Vim plugin and TUI app.
Existing editor-tui foundation contracts remain useful, but are tracked as implementation input rather than completion evidence.
Runtime completion requires executable entrypoints, runnable flows, tests, CI evidence and release artifacts.
